title: The Declaration of Independence
summary: On September 2, 1945, at Ba Dinh Square, Uncle Ho read the Declaration of Independence, giving birth to the Democratic Republic of Vietnam.

slides.text: Tens of thousands of people from everywhere converged on the historic Ba Dinh Square in the joyous victory of the August Revolution.
slides.imagePrompt: Huge crowd of people with red flags at Ba Dinh square, sunny day, ocean of flags, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Uncle Ho, in his simple khaki clothes, stepped onto the high podium and spoke in his warm, dear voice.
slides.imagePrompt: Ho Chi Minh standing at the podium on Ba Dinh square, simple khaki clothes, speaking into a microphone, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: When Uncle Ho asked: 'Can the fellow countrymen hear me clearly?', the whole square replied in unison: 'Yes!', echoing across the sky.
slides.imagePrompt: Uncle Ho smiling and pointing to the crowd, people shouting 'Yes!' in unison, emotional moment, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The declaration of independence opened a new era, an era of freedom and happiness for the entire Vietnamese nation.
slides.imagePrompt: Birds flying over Ba Dinh square, symbol of peace and newly born independence, beautiful sky, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: On which day did Uncle Ho read the Declaration of Independence?
At which square did Uncle Ho read the Declaration of Independence?
